skill.name: frontend-mobile-development-component-scaffold
skill.description: You are a React component architecture expert specializing in scaffolding production-ready, accessible, and performant components. Generate complete component implementations with TypeScript, tests, s
skill.summary: Scaffold production-ready React and React Native components with TypeScript, tests, styles, and accessibility built-in
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static security findings are false positives. The detected patterns (external_commands, weak_crypto, obfuscation) are actually JavaScript template literals and standard array methods used in example code. The skill is a legitimate React component scaffolding tool with no security risks.

content.user_title: Scaffold React Components with TypeScript and Tests
content.value_statement: This skill generates production-ready React and React Native components with full TypeScript typing, accessibility features, testing setup, and multiple styling options. It ensures consistent code quality across your component library.
actual_capabilities: Generate TypeScript React components with proper prop typing and interfaces
Create React Native mobile components with platform-specific patterns
Build comprehensive test suites with Jest and React Testing Library
Produce Storybook stories for component documentation
Implement accessibility (A11y) features with ARIA roles and labels
Support multiple styling approaches: CSS Modules, styled-components, and Tailwind
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files directly - provides code templates only
Requires user to integrate generated code into their project manually
Does not support Vue, Angular, or other frontend frameworks
Does not generate backend logic or API integrations

use_cases.title: Rapid Component prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly generate complete component files with tests and documentation to speed up development workflow
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building new UI features

use_cases.title: Team code standardization
use_cases.description: Ensure all team members create components following consistent patterns and best practices

use_cases.title: Accessibility-first development
use_cases.description: Generate components with built-in accessibility features to meet WCAG compliance requirements

prompt_templates.title: Basic component generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a functional React component called [ComponentName] with props for [prop1], [prop2]. Use TypeScript and CSS Modules for styling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a simple button or input component

prompt_templates.title: Full-featured component with tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete React component with TypeScript, including: component file, prop types, CSS Modules styles, Jest tests with Testing Library, and Storybook story. Make it accessible.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a production-ready form component with validation

prompt_templates.title: React Native mobile component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React Native component for [ComponentName] that works on iOS and Android. Include TypeScript interfaces, StyleSheet styles, and accessibility props.
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a mobile-friendly list item or card component

prompt_templates.title: Component library entry point
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a set of related components for a [ComponentName] feature including: main component, sub-components, types, styles, tests, and index file for clean imports.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a complete feature module like a modal system or data table

output_examples.input: Create a Button component with variant, size, and disabled props
output_examples.output: A complete React component with TypeScript interface, CSS Modules styles, accessibility attributes, and test file

output_examples.input: Generate a Card component for React Native with image, title, and description
output_examples.output: React Native component with StyleSheet, TouchableOpacity, accessibilityLabel, and proper TypeScript types

best_practices: Always use TypeScript interfaces for component props to ensure type safety across your application
Include accessibility attributes from the start rather than adding them later
Generate tests alongside components to maintain high test coverage
anti_patterns: Avoid generating components without prop types - this leads to runtime errors
Do not skip accessibility features as they are harder to add after implementation
Avoid mixing styling approaches in the same component

faq.question: Does this skill create actual files in my project?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates code templates that you can copy and paste into your project. It does not modify your filesystem.

faq.question: Which styling options are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports CSS Modules, styled-components, and Tailwind CSS. Choose the approach that matches your project configuration.

faq.question: Can I use this for React Native projects?
faq.answer: Yes, specify 'native' or 'universal' as the platform in your request to generate React Native compatible code.

faq.question: How do I add custom props to generated components?
faq.answer: Include your prop requirements in the prompt. The skill will generate appropriate TypeScript interfaces for your custom props.

faq.question: Are the generated tests ready to run?
faq.answer: Yes, tests use Jest and React Testing Library with proper setup. You may need to configure your testing environment.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code AI assistants.
